summary: Large item decompilation progress PR that converted several item object files from NonMatching to Matching, added or refined item-specific structs and headers, and cleaned symbol metadata for item data/sdata/sdata2. The author explicitly noted that itlugia and itclimbersice were matching but their sdata2 ordering was still difficult to place. decomp.dev reported +17,508 matched code bytes, +26,716 linked code bytes, and 69 new matches.
what_changed: Moved multiple item translation units from NonMatching to Matching in configure.py: itsscopebeam.c, itclimbersice.c, itlugia.c, itzgshell.c, itklap.c, itoldottosea.c, and itcrazyhandbomb.c.
Made substantial decomp progress across item files including itseakchain, itzgshell, itclimbersstring, it_2E5A, itclimbersice, itlugia, itoldottosea, itklap, itsscopebeam, and itcrazyhandbomb.

Refined item headers with more accurate attributes and item variable layouts, including itClimbersStringAttributes, itSeakChain_ItemVars, itSeakChain_Attrs, itClimbersString_ItemVars, itLugiaAttributes, it_2E5A_SubVars, it_2E5A_TierEntry, it_2E5A_Attrs, itOldottoseaAttributes, and ScopeBeamAttrs.
Changed itSeakChain_Attrs static size assertion from 0x5C to 0x6C after expanding the known fields and adding HSD_Joint pointers.
Reworked it_2E5A data modeling by replacing a pointer-like sub-struct interpretation with an inline sub-struct at ItemVars offset 0x1C and by modeling tiered spawn data as it_2E5A_TierEntry entries.

Updated symbols.txt to correct sizes/scopes and to mark many formerly global it_804D*/it_803F* symbols as local @-number labels, especially for .sdata, .sdata2, strings, floats, doubles, and data tables.
Simplified mpColl_80048844 by removing an unused f32 parameter from both mpcoll.c and mpcoll.h; updated call sites in item code accordingly.
Added a declaration for itZRShell_Logic12_Clanked in itzrshell.h and adjusted small item headers such as itclimbersstring.h, itseakchain.h, itlugia.h, itklap.h, itzgshell.h, and itzrshell.h.
smart_moves: Used configure.py matching-state flips only for item objects that the build/report considered matching, making progress explicit and searchable at the object-file level.
Promoted anonymous padding to typed fields in headers where function bodies proved the accesses, which helped many item functions match without leaving broad unknown byte arrays.
Modeled it_2E5A tier data around the actual inline ItemStateDesc-like layout, including the note that anim_joint through xD84_value form an inline sub-structure passed to Item_80268D34.
Converted address-named globals in symbols.txt to local @ labels where the diff showed local strings/floats/doubles, reducing false global symbol surface and reflecting local compiler-generated data.
Removed the unused mpColl_80048844 argument rather than preserving a fake parameter solely from prior guesses, improving the collision API signature for future call-site matching.
Introduced small helper structure names such as ScopeBeamFloats and ScopeBeamAttrs instead of leaving scope beam data as raw arrays, which makes repeated indexed float usage easier to reason about.
decomp_lessons: When an item file is close to matching, inspect special-attribute layouts first; replacing pads with typed f32, s32, HSD_Joint, HSD_JObj, and nested structs can unlock large blocks of code.

ItemVars comments should record both local struct offsets and Item byte offsets, as seen in the it_2E5A_SubVars and itSeakChain_ItemVars documentation; this helps avoid pointer-vs-inline layout mistakes.
If a function parameter is never used and the matched body supports it, correct the shared header signature and update all call sites instead of carrying an incorrect prototype.
sdata2 ordering can remain a blocker even when code is otherwise matching; the author explicitly called out itlugia and itclimbersice as matching but difficult to order in sdata2.
For data tables embedded inside larger attributes, it may be necessary to model overlapping or inline engine structs rather than inventing separate heap/pointer-backed structs.

Symbol hygiene matters for matching data: local @ labels for compiler-local constants and strings are preferable to preserving address-derived global it_804D*/it_803F* names when scope evidence is local.
naming_conventions: Item motion callbacks continue to use the existing pattern it<ItemName>_UnkMotion<N>_<Anim|Phys|Coll>, e.g. itSscopebeam_UnkMotion9_Coll, itKlap_UnkMotion1_Coll, and itZrshell_UnkMotion11_Anim.
Unknown item functions remain address-named with the it_802xxxxx pattern when no semantic name is established, e.g. it_802E5AC4, it_802D208C, it_802BC080, and it_802C3520.
Struct fields retain offset-derived names such as x0, x4, xDD4_itemVar, xD84_value, and x64_joint when semantics are incomplete, with comments providing offset and usage clues.
Special attribute structs use item-specific names ending in Attributes or Attrs, including itLugiaAttributes, itOldottoseaAttributes, itSeakChain_Attrs, ScopeBeamAttrs, and it_2E5A_Attrs.
Compiler-local data symbols in symbols.txt are represented as @number labels, while still-unknown globals keep address-derived names like it_804DD498 or it_803F89C8.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Used PAD_STACK adjustments and small local padding arrays where stack layout required exact shaping.
Replaced verbose manual absolute-value logic with ABS(...) in it_2E5A collision/velocity code, reducing code shape while preserving matching behavior according to the diff.
Added a static inline helper it_2E5A_ItemStateDesc(Item*) to cast from the tier-entry anim_joint field to ItemStateDesc*, reflecting the underlying data layout expected by item state setup.
Changed mpColl_80048844(CollData*, f32) to mpColl_80048844(CollData*) after confirming the implementation only calls mpCollPrev(coll) and inline4(coll, 0).
Used nested structs and arrays for repeated data patterns, such as it_2E5A_TierEntry tiers[3] and ScopeBeamFloats floats[9], instead of keeping opaque pads.
Adjusted symbol sizes and scopes for .data, .sdata, and .sdata2 to keep data ordering and linkage aligned with newly matching item objects.

review_feedback.feedback: itlugia and itclimbersice were described as matching, but the author could not get their sdata2 in the correct order.
Report showed 69 new matches, +17,508 matched code bytes, +26,716 linked code bytes, +1,528 matched data bytes, and +1,416 linked data bytes.
No human review comments were present in the provided slice.

follow_up_queries: How were itlugia and itclimbersice sdata2 constants eventually ordered after PR 2421?
Which later PR renamed address-based item functions introduced or matched here, such as it_802BC080, it_802E5AC4, or it_802D208C?
Did later work refine itSeakChain_Attrs field names beyond x10/x14/x18 style offset names?
Were the it_2E5A tier-entry and inline ItemStateDesc interpretations reused or corrected in later PRs?
Did any later review alter the mpColl_80048844 signature or reveal additional collision API conventions?
